name: Superb Cash Advance
description_short: Atlanta storefront lender offering payday loans and cash advances up to $1,000 with same-day funding, accepting all credit types including no-credit-check applicants.
description_long:
Superb Cash Advance operates a physical branch at 1919 Metropolitan Pkwy SW in Atlanta, Georgia, providing short-term lending to local residents. The company appears to be a multi-location operation — customer reviews reference a separate Arvada, Colorado branch and a staff member named Helen — suggesting a regional or national franchise structure. The Atlanta storefront maintains extended hours seven days a week, making it more accessible than typical bank or credit union branches.

The core product lineup consists of payday loans and cash advance loans, both capped at $1,000 and targeted at borrowers who need a small amount of cash quickly with minimal paperwork. The company also advertises bad credit loans ranging from $250 to $50,000 for larger borrowing needs, and same-day loans with minimal documentation as a distinct offering. An online application is available for amounts up to $5,000. Customer reviews additionally mention installment loans and second lien title loans, indicating a broader menu than what the primary marketing emphasizes.

Superb Cash Advance accepts all credit types and, according to multiple customer reviews, does not conduct a hard credit check during the approval process. Flexible payment arrangements are a recurring theme in reviews — staff reportedly work with borrowers to design repayment schedules aligned with individual budgets. The extended operating hours (8:30am–8:30pm weekdays, 9:30am–6:30pm weekends) are a genuine differentiator in a sector where storefront access often ends at 5pm.

For Atlanta-area residents with bad or no credit needing fast access to small amounts, Superb Cash Advance is a functional option — but it carries the standard caveats of the payday and cash advance industry. No APR, fee schedules, or loan cost information is disclosed on the location page, which is a meaningful transparency gap; payday and cash advance products routinely carry triple-digit annualized rates. The second lien title loan product mentioned in one review carries compounded risk, as it encumbers a vehicle that is already pledged against existing financing. Additionally, several positive reviews explicitly reference the Arvada location rather than Atlanta, suggesting ratings may be aggregated across all branches rather than reflecting this specific storefront's service quality.
pros: Open 7 days a week with extended hours (until 8:30pm weekdays, 6:30pm weekends)
Accepts all credit types with no hard credit check required (per customer reviews)
Same-day funding available with minimal documentation
Flexible repayment schedules tailored to individual budgets (per multiple reviews)
Wide loan range: cash advances from $250 up to $50,000 for bad credit loans
Physical storefront with in-person service for applicants who prefer face-to-face assistance
Online application available for amounts up to $5,000
cons: No APR, interest rates, or fee disclosures visible on the location page — critical cost information is absent
Payday and cash advance products are capped at $1,000, limiting borrowing power for mid-range needs
Customer reviews reference an Arvada, Colorado location rather than Atlanta, suggesting ratings may be cross-location aggregates rather than branch-specific
Second lien title loans (noted in a review) carry elevated risk as they encumber an already-financed vehicle
No mention of licensing, state regulatory compliance, or consumer protections on the page
best_for: Atlanta-area borrowers with bad or thin credit needing $500–$1,000 same-day
Workers with non-standard schedules who need evening or weekend access to a lending storefront
Borrowers who prefer in-person applications and face-to-face loan processing
Consumers who have been declined elsewhere due to credit history and need a no-check-credit option
diagnosis: Superb Cash Advance is best suited for Atlanta residents with damaged or no credit who need small amounts of cash (under $1,000) on the same day and prefer an in-person application process with flexible hours. The main caveat is a complete lack of disclosed rates and fees — borrowers should expect high-cost payday-style terms and must ask for full APR disclosure before accepting any loan offer.
services: Installment loans with flexible repayment schedules
In-person loan services at Atlanta storefront
